subject: Mozi Minute: Rush Fees (pls read)
body:
Words I like: Urgency on their end means profit on yours.

Mozi Minute: Rush Fee/Speed Pass

Most businesses treat rush requests like a burden. Smart ones treat them like a profit center.

Your eyes should light up when a customer makes an unreasonable request. Because you get to counter with unreasonable pricing to match.

And oftentimes - they're happy to pay it!

On a personal note, I am almost always willing to pay 2-4x more to be first in line or prioritized.

Here's a sample pricing ladder:

- Triple Speed = Triple Price
- Double speed = Double price.
- Standard speed = Standard Price.

Translate it to the timelines that apply to your business. Post it everywhere.

The beauty?

You're not forcing speed - you're pricing choice.

Real World Examples:

Accountant: Tax return in 2 weeks = $500. One week = $750. 48 hours = $1,000.

Sign Shop: Banner in 5 days = $300. 2 days = $450. Today = $600.

Auto Shop: Standard wait = $200. Next day = $300. Within 4 hours = $400.

Don't apologize for these prices. Don't hide them.

Put them on your website. Some clients will always pay more for speed. Your job? Let them.

The profit math is simple:

If they want it faster, make it pricier.

Worst case? They pick normal speed & you make the same money.

Best case? They pay double for the exact same work you were gonna do anyways, you just do the work in a different order.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Speed costs more,

Alex
